sections.title: Domain-driven architecture
sections.body: DomusMind is domain-centric: technology and interfaces are secondary to household invariants and business concepts.

sections.title: Bounded contexts
sections.body: Core contexts include Family, Responsibilities, Calendar, Tasks, Properties, Food, Pets, and Administration.

sections.title: Vertical slices
sections.body: Capabilities are delivered through vertical slices that include request, validation, application logic, persistence interaction, and API endpoint.

sections.title: Command and query model
sections.body: Application behavior uses command/query separation to keep write-side intent and read-side views explicit.

sections.title: Domain events
sections.body: Meaningful changes such as MemberAdded or RoutineTriggered are represented as events for loose coupling and collaboration.

sections.title: Modular monolith
sections.body: The current execution model is modular monolith, enabling strong boundaries while keeping operational complexity manageable.

sections.title: API-first platform
sections.body: Web, mobile, messaging, and automations interact through a capability-oriented API, not direct database exposure.
